notes: Philosopher and physician mentioned by Ammianus Marcellinus and Basil of Caesarea, skilled in persuasion and encouraging truth.

docs.title: Letter 43
docs.author: Julian the Apostate
docs.subject_summary: Perhaps the proverb "An honest man"3—is too hackneyed. I am sure you know the rest. More
docs.excerpt: On your behalf I would strongly maintain that you belong in that category. About myself I say nothing. I only pray that others find by experience that I am honest too.

docs.title: Letter 119
docs.author: Libanius
docs.excerpt: So you, as the most respected of philosophers, should pray to Justice to change their ways. And I, as the clumsiest of ignoramuses, ask the same goddess to keep them exactly as they are.
